title: Increment and Decrement (Ctrl-A / Ctrl-X)
subtitle: Find the next number, add or subtract — with counts.
summary: Ctrl-A increments the next number on the line; Ctrl-X decrements. With a count, it adds/subtracts that much. Combined with macros and :norm, generates sequences instantly.

blocks.text: {key:Ctrl-A} adds 1 to the next number on the line. {key:Ctrl-X} subtracts 1. With a count: add/subtract that count.

rows: Increment next number on line by 1
Increment by count, with PROGRESSIVE step on visual selection

blocks.title: g Ctrl-A and Sequences
blocks.text:
On a *visual selection* of multiple lines, each containing a number:

  • {key:Ctrl-A} adds the count to *every* number.
  • {key:g}{key:Ctrl-A} adds an *increasing* multiple of the count to each line in turn.

Classic recipe: write 100 lines of `0`, select all, press {key:g}{key:Ctrl-A} → you have 1, 2, 3, … 100.

**Number recognition** is controlled by `'nrformats'` — by default decimal and hex (`0x` prefixed) and octal (`0` prefixed; arguably surprising). Set `:set nrformats-=octal` to avoid the gotcha where `08` is treated as malformed octal. (For the broader option system, see Part 21's configuration chapter.)

Vim also recognizes negative signs and will respect them when wrapping (decrementing 0 gives -1).
Need a numbered list? `100i0<Esc>` (insert 100 zeros) then `Vgg` (select all) then `g<Ctrl-A>` (= numbered 1..100).
